channel.title: OnOptions
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from OnOptions, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: My Colleague Lied to Me About a Shared Project
item.description: … and two other tricky workplace dilemmas.

item.title: Anthropic’s Department of War lawsuit is even higher-stakes amid the AI boom
item.description: Anthropic suing the U.S. Department of War perhaps wasn’t a surprise, but it was nevertheless a shock.

item.title: Buc-ee’s is loved by shoppers, so why did it get red-flagged by the Better Business Bureau? Here’s what you need to know
item.description: Loyal customers of Buc-ee’s have made the store a fan favorite, but the nonprofit Better Business Bureau tells another story, recently slapping the po...

item.title: 3 science-backed ways to practice optimism at work (that aren’t phony or forced)
item.description: Optimism has a branding problem at work. It often shows up as pressure to stay upbeat in meetings, reassurance that everything will work out, or encou...

item.title: How camouflage became ‘the original deception’
item.description: It takes a few moments to see the spotted jaguar slinking through the dense, sun-dappled jungle scene projected onto a wall inside the International S...

item.title: Women are still being badly overlooked in hiring
item.description: Despite decades of scientific research , incredible advances in deep analytics and AI , and no shortage of good intentions, many organizations still s...

item.title: Meet Throne Labs, the Company Making Public Bathrooms a Better Place to Go
item.description: After a year of major growth, Jessica Heinzelman’s smart, modular public bathrooms are in 20 cities and counting.

item.title: To Expand Reproductive Health Care Access, Cadence OTC Went to the Convenience Store
item.description: Co-founder Samantha Miller has a simple goal: Make Plan B available everywhere condoms are sold.

item.title: Her Fluorescent Crops Give a Voice to Plants—and Power to Farmers
item.description: Shely Aronov’s InnerPlant has raised $53 million and partnered with John Deere to help plants alert farmers to pests and disease.

item.title: Why blended workforces fail without this new kind of leadership
item.description: Most organisations are no longer made up of one homogenous group of full-time employees. Instead, they’re becoming ecosystems. A mix of permanent empl...

item.title: The AI risk that few organizations are governing
item.description: You know how many human users have access to your financial systems. Do you know how many AI agents do?

item.title: How President Trump Hijacked Oil Markets With Social Media Diplomacy
item.description: Monday saw the most volatile swings in crude prices on record.

item.title: AI isn’t killing jobs yet—CEOs are using layoffs to fund a $2.5 trillion arms race
item.description: Also: All the news and watercooler chat from Fortune.

item.title: AI can double output. Human biology can’t
item.description: There's an AI burnout trap, and your productivity gains are fragile.

item.title: Trump says Iran war is ‘very complete, pretty much’ sparking global stock rally
item.description: But also, "we’re going to go further."
